title: My Fair Laddy

jokes.text: I'm taking a leave of absence. I will return in the fall as Mr. Pommelhorst, your new shop teacher.
jokes.setup: Teacher making emotional announcement about leave of absence
jokes.punchline: Reveals gender transition in matter-of-fact way
jokes.explanation: Progressive humor handled with surprising normalcy for 2006, subverts expectation of typical teacher announcement

jokes.text: We're telling you children now, so you can adjust and not make jokes.
jokes.setup: Principal trying to prevent inappropriate jokes
jokes.punchline: The inevitability of kids making jokes about this situation
jokes.explanation: Classic Skinner naive optimism about controlling student behavior

jokes.text: Looks like there'll be some new wood in shop class!
jokes.setup: Skinner's request not to make jokes
jokes.punchline: Immediate crude double entendre about 'wood'
jokes.explanation: Predictable but perfectly timed innuendo that proves Skinner's point

jokes.text: Precisely the type of wordplay I seek to discourage.
jokes.setup: Student makes crude joke
jokes.punchline: Skinner's overly formal, defeated response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner voice - formal language in defeat

jokes.text: New wood.
jokes.setup: Skinner's disapproval of the wordplay
jokes.punchline: Student repeats it more simply, doubling down
jokes.explanation: Economy of language makes it funnier - just repeating the key phrase

jokes.text: Duck or die!
jokes.setup: Lisa asks about bombardment rules
jokes.punchline: Brutally simple and violent instruction
jokes.explanation: Perfect character establishment - militaristic gym teacher distilled to three words

jokes.text: Bombardment, deal with it. Bombardment, life's a bitch, son. Bombardment! Walk it off. Bombardment! Bombardment!
jokes.setup: Coach established as saying 'bombardment' a lot
jokes.punchline: He literally just says bombardment after every interaction
jokes.explanation: Strong character bit that becomes running gag - absurd repetition

jokes.text: Bombardment!
jokes.setup: Student catches ball and says he's safe
jokes.punchline: Coach hits him anyway while yelling catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Running gag payoff - shows coach ignores actual rules

jokes.text: Well, tell your regular bully. He won't like it one bit.
jokes.setup: Bart says teacher is bullying him
jokes.punchline: Marge suggests reporting to his usual bully
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd logic - treats bullying as territorial, like organized crime

jokes.text: Marge, I ripped my pants, the only pants that understand my complex heinie.
jokes.setup: Homer has wardrobe problem
jokes.punchline: Anthropomorphizes pants as understanding his butt
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - treats clothing as sentient and his body as complex engineering problem

jokes.text: Normal wear and tear.
jokes.setup: Marge asks how pants ripped
jokes.punchline: Cut to Homer clearly crashing go-kart violently
jokes.explanation: Classic visual contradiction - deadpan lie paired with obvious evidence

jokes.text: My ass!
jokes.setup: Says 'normal wear and tear' while crashing
jokes.punchline: Literally referring to his ass being damaged
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - sounds like exclamation but is literal

jokes.text: I paid for 50 laps, and I'm going to take them!
jokes.setup: Go-kart is clearly destroyed
jokes.punchline: Homer insists on continuing despite obvious damage
jokes.explanation: Pure Homer logic - stubborn adherence to getting his money's worth

jokes.text: I'll have to go to every store in the pants district to find another pair like these.
jokes.setup: Homer needs new pants
jokes.punchline: Springfield apparently has a 'pants district'
jokes.explanation: Simpsons world-building - treats shopping like major urban planning

jokes.text: You cry like a little girl, Amy. Come on now; those were just baby teeth.
jokes.setup: Coach continuing bombardment game
jokes.punchline: Dismisses serious injury because teeth were temporary
jokes.explanation: Dark coach humor - twisted logic about dental development

jokes.text: 'Bombardment! Bombardment! How do you like bombardment?' I'll show him.
jokes.setup: Lisa reading peacefully
jokes.punchline: Gets hit and immediately plots revenge
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa character moment - intellectual planning revenge

jokes.text: Why did I put this in here? My saxophone!
jokes.setup: Lisa making ice ball for revenge
jokes.punchline: Discovers she froze her saxophone in the ice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - her signature item accidentally weaponized

jokes.text: But my doctor says no more bombardment. Well, here's a second opinion.
jokes.setup: Milhouse has medical excuse
jokes.punchline: Coach dismisses medical advice for his own 'opinion'
jokes.explanation: Strong medical joke - subverts 'second opinion' concept

jokes.text: Do your worst; I'm drunk. Well, welcome to A.A.: Always attacking.
jokes.setup: Nelson claims to be drunk
jokes.punchline: Coach twists AA into 'Always Attacking'
jokes.explanation: Clever acronym twist that fits character perfectly

jokes.text: Bart's got an ice ball. What is your major malfunction, Simpson?
jokes.setup: Student tattles that Bart has frozen ammunition
jokes.punchline: Coach uses military-style interrogation language
jokes.explanation: Full Metal Jacket reference shows coach's military mindset. Character-appropriate but fairly standard parody.

jokes.text: Me shack, it's ruined! You red rubber home wrecker!
jokes.setup: Dodgeball hits Willie's shack
jokes.punchline: Willie treats ball like a marriage wrecker
jokes.explanation: Great Willie dialogue - treats dodgeball like domestic saboteur

jokes.text: Don't be tantalizing me with tales of ravines and rusted-out cars. I'll only be getting me heart broke.
jokes.setup: Bart offers to help with junk for new shack
jokes.punchline: Willie treats garbage like romantic temptation
jokes.explanation: Exceptional Willie dialogue - treats junk hunting like dangerous romance, perfect character voice

jokes.text: I got to go. I finished nailing this board. Yeah, you're out of nails.
jokes.setup: Willie trying to rebuild shack
jokes.punchline: Single board nailed up used all available nails
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy showing Willie's construction incompetence

jokes.text: I don't need your charity, not as long as I've got a pan over me head.
jokes.setup: Marge offers Willie shelter
jokes.punchline: Willie's 'shelter' is clearly inadequate cookware
jokes.explanation: Classic Willie pride despite obvious inadequacy

jokes.text: That's not a pan, it's a colander.
jokes.setup: Willie claims pan provides shelter
jokes.punchline: It's full of holes
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline - makes Willie's situation even more pathetic

jokes.text: It's a sculpture made of children's lost retainers.
jokes.setup: Willie offers gift to Simpsons
jokes.punchline: His 'art' is made from dental appliances
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly gross and specific - perfectly Willie gift

jokes.text: It's lovely. I like this part in here, the way it, it goes.
jokes.setup: Marge trying to be polite about disgusting gift
jokes.punchline: Struggles to find anything positive to say
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge politeness struggling with horrifying reality

jokes.text: Me grandfather used to get sent down into the mines to make sure it was safe for the canaries.
jokes.setup: Willie explaining his family's acceptance of squalor
jokes.punchline: His grandfather was considered less valuable than canaries
jokes.explanation: Exceptional dark comedy - inverts coal mine safety protocol, making humans less valuable than warning birds. Peak Willie family dysfunction.

jokes.text: That's it.
jokes.setup: Sings about wanting 'a place somewhere'
jokes.punchline: His entire aspiration is just basic shelter
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing - two words deflate entire musical number

jokes.text: To have me shack rebuilt get my rotten teeth all drill't something on underneath me kilt wouldn't it be adequate?
jokes.setup: Willie tries to dream bigger
jokes.punchline: His aspirations are still depressingly basic
jokes.explanation: Great character song - Willie's 'big dreams' are just basic hygiene and shelter

jokes.text: I'm too far off the ground! Lisa, come up here and help me down!
jokes.setup: Willie trying to stand up straight
jokes.punchline: Terrified by normal human posture
jokes.explanation: Funny physical comedy - Willie so used to hunching he fears upright posture

jokes.text: Big blue pants. When you no longer care if you're attractive to women.
jokes.setup: Commercial for Homer's beloved pants
jokes.punchline: Marketed as giving up on romance entirely
jokes.explanation: Brilliant anti-marketing - brutally honest about product's appeal

jokes.text: I'll kill ya! I'll kill ya for what ya done to me!
jokes.setup: Lisa asks how to address an archbishop
jokes.punchline: Willie's answer is violent threats
jokes.explanation: Shows Willie's resistance to refinement through violent response to etiquette

jokes.text: You were born trash, and you'll be lucky if you grow up to be garbage!
jokes.setup: Willie explaining his father's lack of encouragement
jokes.punchline: Father thinks garbage would be upward mobility from trash
jokes.explanation: Exceptional dark comedy - treats garbage as aspirational upgrade from trash. Perfect Willie family psychology.

jokes.text: Would you like to cut the cord? Let him cut it himself, it's time he learned life ain't one big party!
jokes.setup: Doctor offers father chance to cut umbilical cord
jokes.punchline: Father refuses, wants baby to face harsh reality immediately
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - applies harsh parenting philosophy to literal birth moment. Peak Willie family dysfunction.

jokes.text: That's as unlikely as Kearney going around the world in 80 days.
jokes.setup: Homer dismisses Lisa's bet as impossible
jokes.punchline: Uses literary reference that Kearney immediately accepts
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Kearney gag - literary reference backfires

jokes.text: Eighty days... ample time to circum navigate the globe, booger breath. You, sir, have a wager!
jokes.setup: Homer mentions 80 days around world
jokes.punchline: Kearney is unexpectedly erudite about it
jokes.explanation: Excellent character subversion - bully Kearney shows unexpected literary knowledge and formal speech

jokes.text: Wrong way, dingus!
jokes.setup: Kearney dramatically heads off on his journey
jokes.punchline: Immediately goes the wrong direction
jokes.explanation: Quick deflation of Kearney's dramatic exit

jokes.text: It's called 'headvertising.' It creates brand awareness without relying on traditional media.
jokes.setup: Marge asks about thing on Homer's head
jokes.punchline: Homer has logo tattooed/painted on his head for advertising
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay and concept - Homer as human billboard

jokes.text: It's not supposed to.
jokes.setup: Marge notes the logo glows in the dark
jokes.punchline: Homer realizes something went wrong with the process
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - suggests Homer's head advertising went medically wrong

jokes.text: I'm not very cultured. I don't even have a last name!
jokes.setup: Willie frustrated with lessons
jokes.punchline: Claims his lack of surname proves uncultured status
jokes.explanation: Excellent Willie logic - treats lack of last name as cultural deficiency

jokes.text: A talking mirror!
jokes.setup: Willie practicing pronunciation
jokes.punchline: Amazed by his own reflection speaking properly
jokes.explanation: Good Willie moment - so unused to proper speech he doesn't recognize himself

jokes.text: Meanwhile, I been paying for that billboard outside for a year now, and it's not yieldin' nothin'!
jokes.setup: Moe watching Homer's head advertising succeed
jokes.punchline: His traditional billboard advertising has failed completely
jokes.explanation: Good Moe complaint - his business struggles with conventional advertising

jokes.text: I have the strangest feeling I've met him before.
jokes.setup: Willie introduced as sophisticated G.K. Willington
jokes.punchline: Selma almost recognizes him but doesn't quite
jokes.explanation: Standard dramatic irony - audience knows what character doesn't

jokes.text: And you may meet him again, as your new father!
jokes.setup: Selma expressing mild interest in Willie
jokes.punchline: Patty immediately imagines marriage scenario
jokes.explanation: Good Patty character moment - jumps immediately to family implications

jokes.text: If you ask me, the royal shakespeare company's latest season was much ado about nothing.
jokes.setup: Willie demonstrates his new sophistication
jokes.punchline: Makes Shakespeare pun while referencing actual play title
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay showing Willie's transformation. The pun is obvious but works in context of his learning.

jokes.text: Well, mother, aren't you glad you didn't get more intimate with him?
jokes.setup: Willie revealed as groundskeeper to shocked crowd
jokes.punchline: Lisa asks if Selma is relieved about avoiding intimacy
jokes.explanation: Good callback to romantic tension with dark implications

jokes.text: For goodness sakes! How did I get up here?!
jokes.setup: New groundskeeper doing Willie's old job
jokes.punchline: Somehow got stuck high up and confused about it
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy showing new groundskeeper's incompetence

jokes.text: What's the 'd' for? Dimwit?
jokes.setup: Willie introduces himself as maitre d'
jokes.punchline: Customer assumes 'd' stands for insult
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Willie's service industry struggles

jokes.text: I don't need your charity titters. Now, say hello to my girlfriend, Charity Titters.
jokes.setup: Customer makes rude joke
jokes.punchline: His girlfriend's name is literally 'Charity Titters'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay - turns insult into actual character name

jokes.text: Ay! El hombre invisible lleva pantalones azules!
jokes.setup: Blue pants trend has spread everywhere
jokes.punchline: Even Bumblebee Man reports on invisible man wearing blue pants
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation of blue pants trend. Invisible man wearing visible pants is paradoxically logical. Good character use.

jokes.text: These rolls are stale! Bombardment! Bring me some more! Bombardment! And, some iced tea for the lady, please. Bombardment!
jokes.setup: Coach Krupt as restaurant customer
jokes.punchline: Still uses 'bombardment' catchphrase for ordering food
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback - shows coach can't turn off his aggressive teaching style

jokes.text: If I had your voice, I'd talk-sing everything.
jokes.setup: Willie singing about missing his shack
jokes.punchline: Barfly comments on Willie's singing voice
jokes.explanation: Meta-comment on musical theater conventions

jokes.text: Dessert tray, that's a great gag. Can I use it? I'm taking it anyway!
jokes.setup: Willie offers dessert tray after outburst
jokes.punchline: Customer treats it like comedy bit and steals the concept
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about comedy writing - character acknowledges funny moment

jokes.text: Heaven, I'm in heaven...
jokes.setup: Willie back to groundskeeping
jokes.punchline: Sings happily while cleaning up garbage
jokes.explanation: Sweet Willie character moment - truly happy doing menial work

jokes.text: I liked it the way it was!
jokes.setup: Lisa gives Willie nice new shack
jokes.punchline: Willie preferred his previous squalor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Willie ending - rejects improvement in favor of familiar misery

tmdb_overview: Mr. Krupt is the school’s new gym teacher and introduces Bart and the kids to “BOMBARDMENT!” Bart’s attempt to get back at the new gym teacher results in the destruction of Groundskeeper Willie’s shack and the family takes him in. Lisa decides to make Willie the focus of her science fair project and tries to turn him into a “proper gentleman” with surprising results. Meanwhile, Homer has ruined his last pair of blue pants and attempts to generate interest in them so the manufacturer will start making them again.
